chief_complaint: A 66-year-old woman presented with persistent cough and dyspnoea after undergoing right inferior lobectomy for pulmonary carcinoma.
history: The patient had a history of pulmonary carcinoma and underwent right inferior lobectomy, but developed a persistent bronchopleural fistula (BPF) that was not amenable to surgical intervention after two failed surgeries.
exam: No specific vital signs or physical examination findings are mentioned.
labs: No specific investigations or results are mentioned, but the patient underwent a hybrid procedure using fluoroscopy and bronchoscopy.
